question: [File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/NodeSignatureVerifier.java -> Scope: High: Temporary freezing of network transactions by delaying one block by 500% or more of the average block time of the preceding 24 hours beyond standard difficulty adjustments'] [Function: verify()] Can an unprivileged external user inject a crafted collection of StreamFileSignature objects where every entry has a null publicKey, causing all verifySignature() calls to return false and consensusValidator.validate() to receive zero VERIFIED signatures, throwing SignatureVerificationException and permanently stalling block ingestion beyond 500% of average block time?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/NodeSignatureVerifier.java -> Scope: High: Temporary freezing of network transactions by delaying one block by 500% or more of the average block time of the preceding 24 hours beyond standard difficulty adjustments'] [Function: verifySignature()] Can an unprivileged external user supply a StreamFileSignature with a null fileHashSignature field, causing verifySignature() to log an error and return false for every node's signature, resulting in no VERIFIED entries reaching consensusValidator.validate() and blocking block processing for a duration exceeding 500% of average block time?
